reasoning_model_1: The model successfully generates the full text script, albeit with a minor addition of 'Shh' at the very beginning (00:00). This addition, while strictly a deviation from the text content, serves as a strong paralinguistic marker that reinforces the requested 'whisper' style and the 'secretive' context of the prompt. The pacing is natural, covering the full sentence in a reasonable timeframe (00:00-00:08). The inclusion of the hushed 'Shh' suggests the model successfully modulated the volume and tone to match the 'whisper' instruction throughout the utterance.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails significantly in Content Accuracy. The generated audio serves only the first half of the sentence, cutting off abruptly after 'play games' (around 00:07) and completely omitting the segment 'mom and dad don't know, you absolutely mustn't tell them.' Additionally, the timestamps indicate significant gaps or a very slow pace (e.g., silence between 00:00 and 00:02, and 00:02 and 00:04), which may impair Fluency and Naturalness. Due to the incomplete generation, the model fails to deliver the full performance required to evaluate the 'whisper' feature effectively.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It provides a complete and fluent generation that adheres to the user's intent. The addition of 'Shh' effectively sets the stage for the requested 'whisper' feature, enhancing the expressiveness. In contrast, Model 2 produces a truncated audio file that misses half of the required text. According to the evaluation criteria, a major content omission prevents the model from achieving a high score, regardless of the vocal characteristics of the generated segment.
